chunks.label: Preparing for class and studying languages including Spanish

top_match.sentence_text: I was I was listening to about about English after our class, so I wanted to to to to prepare prepare some of it.
top_match.explanation: Infinitive vs gerund — the same verb changes meaning depending on whether it is followed by 'to' or '-ing' (e.g. 'stop to think' vs 'stop thinking').

chunks.label: Explaining decision to speak spontaneously without preparation

top_match.sentence_text: I think I think it's I think it wouldn't be okay if I I had read, for for example, after after telling you.
top_match.explanation: Impersonal passive with 'it' — 'it is said/reported/believed that' to describe general opinion or formally attributed information (e.g. 'It is believed that the building dates from 1800').

chunks.label: Practicing speaking slowly and describing daily activities

top_match.sentence_text: so I should do what what what I've done, but more slowly but about about this topic about this topic, about football, about what what you answered me.
top_match.explanation: 'Should be' + continuous — describes what is expected to be happening right now (e.g. 'You should be studying').

chunks.label: Discussing lunch, healthy eating habits, and daily activities

top_match.sentence_text: I have to, you know, I have to I have to convince myself because I've never done it.
top_match.explanation: Impersonal passive with 'it' — 'it is said/reported/believed that' to describe general opinion or formally attributed information (e.g. 'It is believed that the building dates from 1800').

chunks.label: Healthy eating habits, preferences, and daily plans

top_match.sentence_text: Because I didn't understand because I I didn't have the good.
top_match.explanation: Past tense in 'because' clauses — past verb in a reason clause explaining why something happened (e.g. 'She left because she was tired').

chunks.label: Daily plans, preferences, and asking teacher questions

top_match.sentence_text: Do you know what do you know about languages?
top_match.explanation: Stranded preposition — prepositional verb where the preposition is separated from its object, often at end of clause (e.g. 'the problem I was talking about').

chunks.label: Reflecting on class, learning progress, and future goals

top_match.sentence_text: So I think I've learned about breakfast about breakfast, about what what I ate, what I could could tell you, and I like it because I think it's important to to keep speaking, to keep to keep having vocabulary a vocab the the new vocabulary.
top_match.explanation: Impersonal passive with 'it' — 'it is said/reported/believed that' to describe general opinion or formally attributed information (e.g. 'It is believed that the building dates from 1800').

chunks.label: Reflecting on learning goals and closing the class
